task: I need to answer a company’s follow-up about a billing issue, and I want the reply to be clear and easy to use. Can you help me draft something I can send without a lot of back-and-forth?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a short, firm reply you can send:

> I dispute this billing issue and do not agree to any changes or charges. Please provide the exact basis for the billing in writing, including the dates, amounts, and any supporting documents. Until I receive that information and agree in writing, no changes or charges are authorized.

If you want, I can also make it:
- more firm,
- more polite, or
- tailored to email vs. letter.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty good, but I’d want it a bit tighter and more specific. Right now it’s still a little generic.

Make it say I want the invoice corrected or broken down line by line, and that I’m not discussing payment until they send the documentation. Also mention I want everything in writing. I don’t need the soft options — just the version I can send.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version you can send:

> I dispute this invoice as currently billed. Please send a corrected invoice or a line-by-line breakdown showing the basis for each charge, including dates, amounts, and supporting documentation. I will not discuss payment until I receive that information. All further communication on this issue must be in writing.

If you want, I can also make it a little firmer or turn it into a shorter email subject + body.

user_simulator.text: That’ll do. Tight enough, and it says what needs saying. I can use that.
